---
title: "Linux Setup Guide"
description: "Install Git, configure your terminal, and set up SSH authentication on Linux."
platform: linux
---

## What You Will Install

| Tool | Version | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| Git | ≥ 2.28 | Version control |
| OpenSSH client | Latest | SSH key authentication |

## Step 1: Open Terminal

Most Linux desktop environments provide a terminal via **Ctrl + Alt + T** or by
searching "Terminal" in your application launcher.

## Step 2: Install Git

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Ubuntu / Debian

```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install git
```

## Fedora / RHEL

```bash
sudo dnf install git
```

## Arch Linux

```bash
sudo pacman -S git
```
:::

## Step 3: Verify Installation

```bash
git --version
```

```text
git version 2.43.0
```

Anything ≥ 2.28 is supported by this course.

## Step 4: Configure Git

```bash
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "your@institution.edu"
git config --global init.defaultBranch main
git config --global core.editor nano
```

Verify:

```bash
git config --list
```

```text
user.name=Your Name
user.email=your@institution.edu
init.defaultBranch=main
core.editor=nano
```

## Step 5: Generate an SSH Key

```bash
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your@institution.edu"
```

Press **Return** to accept the default file location. Enter a passphrase (recommended).

```text
Generating public/private ed25519 key pair.
Enter file in which to save the key (/home/yourname/.ssh/id_ed25519):
Your identification has been saved in /home/yourname/.ssh/id_ed25519
Your public key has been saved in /home/yourname/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
```

## Step 6: Add SSH Key to GitHub

```bash
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
```

Copy the output. In GitHub: **Settings → SSH and GPG keys → New SSH key** → paste and save.

## Step 7: Verify SSH Connection

```bash
ssh -T git@github.com
```

```text
Hi username! You've successfully authenticated, but GitHub does not provide shell access.
```

## Troubleshooting

**`git: command not found`**
: Installation may not have completed. Re-run the install command, or check `echo $PATH` to ensure `/usr/bin` is present.

**`Permission denied (publickey)`**
: Run `ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519` to load your key into the agent, then retry.

**Git version too old (< 2.28)**
: On older Ubuntu LTS releases, add the Git PPA: `sudo add-apt-repository ppa:git-core/ppa && sudo apt update && sudo apt install git`
